name: Interferon Alfa-2b, Recombinant
description: Interferon alpha 2b (human leukocyte clone hif-sn 206 protein moiety reduced). A type I interferon consisting of 165 amino acid residues with arginine in position 23. This protein is produced by recombinant DNA technology and resembles interferon secreted by leukocytes. It is used extensively as an antiviral or antineoplastic agent.
indication: For the treatment of hairy cell leukemia, malignant melanoma, and AIDS-related Kaposi's sarcoma.
toxicity: There is limited experience with overdosage. Postmarketing surveillance includes reports of patients receiving a single dose as great as 10 times the recommended dose. In general, the primary effects of an overdose are consistent with the effects seen with therapeutic doses of interferon alfa-2b. Hepatic enzyme abnormalities, renal failure, hemorrhage, and myocardial infarction have been reported with single administration overdoses and/or with longer durations of treatment than prescribed. Toxic effects after ingestion of interferon alfa-2b are not expected because interferons are poorly absorbed orally.
mechanismOfAction: Interferon alpha binds to type I interferon receptors (IFNAR1 and IFNAR2c) which upon dimerization activate two Jak (Janus kinase) tyrosine kinases (Jak1 and Tyk2). These transphosphorylate themselves and phosphorylate the receptors. The phosphorylated INFAR receptors then bind to Stat1 and Stat2 (signal transducers and activators of transcription)which dimerize and activate multiple (~100) immunomodulatory and antiviral proteins. Interferon alpha binds less stably to type I interferon receptors than interferon beta.
pharmacodynamics: Upregulates the expression of MHC I proteins, allowing for increased presentation of peptides derived from viral antigens. This enhances the activation of CD8+ T cells that are the precursors for cytotoxic T lymphocytes (CTLs) and makes the macrophage a better target for CTL-mediated killing. Interferon alpha also induce the synthesis of several key antiviral mediators, including 2'-5' oligoadenylate synthetase (2'-5' A synthetase) and protein kinase R.

interactions.name: Telbivudine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Interferon Alfa-2b, Recombinant is combined with Telbivudine.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Interferon Alfa-2b, Recombinant.

interactions.name: Aldesleukin
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Interferon Alfa-2b, Recombinant is combined with Aldesleukin.

interactions.name: Tizanidine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Tizanidine can be increased when it is combined with Interferon Alfa-2b, Recombinant.
